title: Templates
text: :DevcontainerTemplate pulls the catalog from devcontainers/templates, presents it via vim.ui.select, and scaffolds the chosen template into the current workspace’s .devcontainer/.

title: Templates
text:
Cache
The catalog and per-template files are cached on disk under stdpath('cache') .. '/devcontainer.nvim/templates/' with a 24h TTL. On fetch failure the existing cache is reused with a “stale catalog” notify; missing cache + no network is a hard error. Existing .devcontainer/ files are never overwritten.
See ADR-0008 — Template catalog cached from the GitHub API (ADR text lives alongside the source for now).

title: Variants
text:
Some workspaces ship multiple devcontainer.json files side by side, typically split by hardware target:
When more than one config exists, :DevcontainerUp (and :DevcontainerRebuild) prompt via vim.ui.select to pick one. The choice is cached per workspace for the rest of the session and passed to the CLI via --config. Use :DevcontainerVariant to re-pick at any time, or set config_path in setup() (or a project .nvim.lua) to skip the picker entirely:
The same selection is used by lsp_cmd, so the in-container LSP runs against the variant you picked.

title: Variants
text:
Commands







Command
Description




:DevcontainerUp
Build and start the devcontainer. Prompts for a variant if more than one is present.


:DevcontainerVariant
Re-pick the variant for this workspace.


:DevcontainerRebuild
Rebuild with --build-no-cache, reusing the current variant.

title: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present
text: The public lsp_cmd wrapper (require('devcontainer').lsp_cmd(...)) is meant to be the only line users add to their vim.lsp.config. Users frequently work across mixed workspaces — some with a .devcontainer/, some without — and the config should not have to branch on that.
crumbs: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present

title: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present
text: The public lsp_cmd wrapper (require('devcontainer').lsp_cmd(...)) is meant to be the only line users add to their vim.lsp.config. Users frequently work across mixed workspaces — some with a .devcontainer/, some without — and the config should not have to branch on that.
crumbs: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present

title: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present
text:
Decision
When config.find_config() returns nil (no .devcontainer/devcontainer.json and no .devcontainer.json under the workspace folder), lsp_cmd(argv) returns argv unchanged. nvim’s LSP client spawns the host binary directly.
When a config is found, lsp_cmd(argv) returns a function suitable for cmd = function(dispatchers) ... end, which delegates to devcontainer.lsp.proxy.start.
crumbs: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present

title: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present
text:
Alternatives considered

Error / require explicit host config — rejected: forces users to maintain two parallel vim.lsp.config entries.
Always proxy — rejected: would fail on every machine without .devcontainer/.
crumbs: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present

title: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present
text:
Consequences

Positive: the one-liner in the docs works verbatim in every repo.
Negative: silent fallback can surprise users whose .devcontainer/ is outside the workspace root. :checkhealth devcontainer already flags that.
Affects: lua/devcontainer/init.lua (lsp_cmd), the LSP setup example.
crumbs: ADR-0006: lsp_cmd() returns raw argv when no devcontainer.json is present

title: Troubleshooting
text: Run :checkhealth devcontainer first. It probes the CLI, the container runtime, and devcontainer.json parsing, with a distinct message per failure mode.

title: Troubleshooting
text:
devcontainer CLI not on PATH
npm install -g @devcontainers/cli
If you keep the binary somewhere non-standard, set cli = "/path/to/devcontainer" in setup(). Re-run :checkhealth devcontainer to confirm.

title: Troubleshooting
text:
Container runtime daemon not reachable
:checkhealth devcontainer reports container runtime present but 'info' failed; daemon may be down. Start the daemon:
# docker
sudo systemctl start docker

# podman (rootless)
systemctl --user start podman.socket
Verify with docker info or podman info, then re-run :checkhealth devcontainer.

title: Troubleshooting
text:
devcontainer.json parse errors
The parser accepts JSONC (line/block comments, trailing commas on objects). Trailing commas inside arrays and unquoted keys are still rejected. On a parse error :checkhealth devcontainer prints the file path and the underlying error; :DevcontainerStatus also surfaces the resolved config path so you can confirm which file is being read.

title: Troubleshooting
section: LSP attaches but go-to-definition opens an empty buffer
text:
LSP attaches but go-to-definition opens an empty buffer
The proxy maps file:// URIs between host paths and workspaceFolder paths, and exposes container-side paths outside the workspace under the docker://<container_id>/<abs-container-path> scheme. If a system header opens empty, check:

:DevcontainerStatus — the container must be running.
The buffer name should start with docker://. If it shows a host path that does not exist, the workspace folder resolution is off; see LSP in the container.

title: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers devcontainer up, outbound frames queue
text: The RPC proxy rejects start when the cached container state reports running == false. For the one-liner UX promise, the user must never have to run :DevcontainerUp manually — opening a file should be enough.
crumbs: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers `devcontainer up`, outbound frames queue

title: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers devcontainer up, outbound frames queue
text: The RPC proxy rejects start when the cached container state reports running == false. For the one-liner UX promise, the user must never have to run :DevcontainerUp manually — opening a file should be enough.
crumbs: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers `devcontainer up`, outbound frames queue

title: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers devcontainer up, outbound frames queue
text:
Decision
When lsp_cmd() is invoked and the container is not running, the returned cmd function:

Resolves the eventual container workspaceFolder up front by parsing devcontainer.json (existing lua/devcontainer/json.lua + variable substitution).
Calls container.up({ on_exit = ... }).
Returns a client wrapper that buffers outbound request / notify calls in an in-memory queue keyed by insertion order.
On up’s on_exit(state, nil), spawns the real proxy with the cached container_id / remote_workspace_folder and drains the queue into the live client.
On up’s on_exit(_, err), calls dispatchers.on_error(0, err) for each pending request id and dispatchers.on_exit(1, 0).

This behaviour is gated behind auto_up = true in setup().
crumbs: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers `devcontainer up`, outbound frames queue

title: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers devcontainer up, outbound frames queue
text:
Alternatives considered

Refuse until :DevcontainerUp — rejected: breaks the one-liner promise.
Block synchronously on first request — rejected: would freeze the editor for tens of seconds during image build.
crumbs: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers `devcontainer up`, outbound frames queue

title: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers devcontainer up, outbound frames queue
text:
Consequences

Positive: users never see the container plumbing for the happy path.
Negative: an unbounded queue while up runs. Acceptable since LSP startup traffic is small (initialize + initialized + a few didOpen); document as a known limit and consider a hard cap in a future ADR if needed.
Affects: lua/devcontainer/init.lua (lsp_cmd), lua/devcontainer/lsp/proxy.lua (queue-and-drain entry point).
crumbs: ADR-0007: First LSP attach triggers `devcontainer up`, outbound frames queue

title: devcontainer.nvim
text:
Keep Neovim running on the host while LSP servers run inside the Dev Container. An in-process RPC proxy rewrites file:// URIs in JSON-RPC traffic so both sides see correct paths for their environment. Setup is one line in your vim.lsp.config.
flowchart LR
    subgraph Host[Host nvim]
        client["LSP client"] <-- RPC --> proxy["RPC proxy<br/>(path mapping)"]
    end
    proxy <-- stdio over devcontainer exec --> server["LSP server"]
    subgraph Container[devcontainer]
        server <--> fs["workspaceFolder + system files"]
    end

title: devcontainer.nvim
text:
Why this plugin

Host editor. Your nvim config, plugins, fonts, and keybindings stay on the host. Nothing extra to install inside the container.
Transparent LSP. devcontainer.lsp_cmd(argv) builds a cmd value for vim.lsp.config that spawns the language server inside the container and pipes JSON-RPC through a path-rewriting proxy. Same buffer, same diagnostics, container-side dependencies.
Native devcontainer.json parsing. JSONC + variable substitution, not a shell-out. :checkhealth devcontainer validates the file before you hit a startup error.

title: devcontainer.nvim
text:
Requirements

Neovim >= 0.11 (vim.lsp.config / vim.lsp.enable API).
@devcontainers/cli on $PATH (npm install -g @devcontainers/cli).
A container runtime: docker or podman.
A .devcontainer/devcontainer.json (or .devcontainer.json) in the workspace. Multiple variants under .devcontainer/<name>/devcontainer.json (e.g. cpu, gpu) are also supported — see Variants.

title: devcontainer.nvim
devcontainer.nvim
text:
Quickstart
local lsp_cmd = require("devcontainer").lsp_cmd

vim.lsp.config("clangd", {
  cmd = lsp_cmd({ "clangd", "--background-index", "--clang-tidy" }),
  filetypes = { "c", "cpp", "objc", "objcpp", "cuda" },
  root_markers = { "compile_commands.json", ".clangd", ".git" },
})

vim.lsp.enable({ "clangd" })
See LSP in the container for the full story, including auto_up and the RPC proxy internals.

title: devcontainer.nvim
text:
Next steps

LSP in the container — lsp_cmd, the RPC proxy, auto_up.
Variants — multiple devcontainer.json files per workspace.
Templates — scaffold from the official templates catalog.
Troubleshooting — :checkhealth and common errors.
ADRs — architectural decisions.

title: devcontainer.nvim
text:
License
MIT

title: LSP in the container
text: require("devcontainer").lsp_cmd(server_argv) returns a cmd value suitable for vim.lsp.config. When the current workspace has a devcontainer.json, it routes the LSP through the in-container server; otherwise it returns server_argv unchanged so nvim spawns the language server on the host. The same snippet works in both projects.

title: LSP in the container
LSP in the container
LSP in the container
text:
Graceful host fallback
lsp_cmd is the only line you add to vim.lsp.config. When the workspace has no devcontainer.json, the same cmd value spawns the host binary directly — no branching in your config. See ADR-0006.

title: LSP in the container
text:
Setup options
require("devcontainer").setup({
  cli = "devcontainer",      -- path to the devcontainer CLI binary
  workspace_folder = nil,    -- override workspace folder (defaults to cwd)
  config_path = nil,         -- force a specific devcontainer.json (skips the variant picker)
  auto_attach = true,        -- focus the terminal split when commands run
  auto_up = false,           -- implicitly `devcontainer up` on first LSP attach
})








Option
Default
Description




cli
"devcontainer"
Path to the devcontainer CLI binary.


workspace_folder
nil
Override workspace folder. Defaults to cwd.


config_path
nil
Absolute path to a specific devcontainer.json. Skips the variant picker.


auto_attach
true
Focus the terminal split when :DevcontainerExec / :DevcontainerShell opens.


auto_up
false
If true, the first LSP attach triggers devcontainer up and queues RPC until ready.
